_meta.title: Pathogen Surveillance Evaluation Framework (PSEF) v3.0.0 — Canonical Reference Baseline
_meta.description: This is the AUTHORITATIVE reference dataset. All future analysis, scope refinements, and dashboard versions MUST use this as the ground truth. Do NOT delete or modify this file — create new versions instead.

platforms.name: DARPA P3 (Pandemic Prevention Platform)
platforms.description: DARPA program to develop platform that can halt any viral pandemic within 60 days using nucleic acid-encoded medical countermeasures

platforms.name: DARPA PREEMPT
platforms.description: PREventing EMerging Pathogenic Threats - aims to predict and prevent zoonotic spillover events before they reach human populations

platforms.name: DARPA ADEPT
platforms.description: Autonomous Diagnostics to Enable Prevention and Therapeutics - rapid troop-level diagnostic platform development

platforms.name: DARPA NOW
platforms.description: Nucleic acids On-demand Worldwide - mobile platform for rapid field production of nucleic acid medical countermeasures

platforms.name: DARPA INTERCEPT
platforms.description: INTERfering and Co-Evolving Prevention and Therapy - uses viral evolution to create adaptive medical countermeasures

platforms.name: DARPA Safe Genes
platforms.description: Program creating biological capabilities to protect against accidental or intentional misuse of genome editing technologies

platforms.name: DARPA ECHO
platforms.description: Epigenetic CHaracterization and Observation - detects prior WMD exposure through epigenetic signatures

platforms.name: DARPA RTA
platforms.description: Rapid Threat Assessment - speeds production of medical countermeasures for US forces by rapidly characterizing unknown bio-agents

platforms.name: BSVE (Biosurveillance Ecosystem)
platforms.description: DTRA Biosurveillance Ecosystem - multi-source disease forecasting platform correlating epidemiological, environmental, and social data for military operations

platforms.name: GEIS (Global Emerging Infections Surveillance)
platforms.description: Defense Health Agency program providing global biosurveillance through network of military laboratories in 30+ countries since 1997

platforms.name: F-FAST / FFBS
platforms.description: DEVCOM CBC/DTRA far-forward portable nanopore sequencing system for pathogen-agnostic identification in field operations

platforms.name: USAMRIID Biosurveillance
platforms.description: US Army Medical Research Institute of Infectious Diseases - premier military biodefense laboratory at Fort Detrick with BSL-4 capabilities

platforms.name: JBPDS (Joint Biological Point Detection System)
platforms.description: JPEO-CBRND automated airborne biological agent detection system deployed on ships and installations for continuous monitoring

platforms.name: DTRA BTRP
platforms.description: Biological Threat Reduction Program - strengthens partner nation biosurveillance and biosecurity across 30+ countries to prevent BW proliferation

platforms.name: NBIC (National Biosurveillance Integration Center)
platforms.description: DHS center integrating human, animal, plant, and environmental biosurveillance data through Biofeeds system for national biodefense

platforms.name: BioWatch
platforms.description: DHS nationwide air-sampling network in 30+ US cities for early detection of aerosolized biological attacks

platforms.name: NBACC
platforms.description: DHS National Biodefense Analysis and Countermeasures Center - BSL-3/4 facility for bio-threat characterization and bioforensic analysis

platforms.name: IARPA B24IC
platforms.description: IARPA Biointelligence & Biosecurity Innovation Center - developing methods to detect, collect, analyze & prevent biological threats including AI-bio risks

platforms.name: Project BioShield / BARDA
platforms.description: HHS/ASPR program providing $5.6B+ in funding to incentivize private-sector MCM development against CBRN threats

platforms.name: GHS Index
platforms.description: NTI & Johns Hopkins benchmarking of 195 countries on health security and biodefense preparedness across 6 categories

platforms.name: SecureBio / NAO
platforms.description: SecureBio nonprofit developing pathogen-agnostic metagenomic early warning and LLM-based AI-bio risk assessment tools

platforms.name: BioFire Defense (FilmArray)
platforms.description: bioMérieux defense division providing FDA-cleared rapid multiplex PCR panels for field-forward biothreat agent identification

platforms.name: Battelle REBS+
platforms.description: Battelle Resource Effective Bio-identification System Plus - next-generation airborne biological agent identification under $93M DTRA contract (2026)

platforms.name: Teledyne FLIR IBAC 2
platforms.description: Teledyne FLIR Instantaneous Bioaerosol Analysis and Collection system detecting all four classes of biological threats (spore, viral, cellular, toxin)

platforms.name: Smiths Detection BioFlash
platforms.description: Smiths Detection BioFlash Biological Identifier - rapid, sensitive identification of biothreat agents and common diseases using CANARY technology

platforms.name: Bertin Environics ENVI Assay
platforms.description: Finnish military-grade lab-in-a-box biodefense immunochromatographic test system for 7 biological warfare agents including anthrax, ricin, botulinum

platforms.name: Rheinmetall NBC Field Lab
platforms.description: Rheinmetall mobile NBC field laboratories for military biological agent analysis and CBRN reconnaissance deployed by Bundeswehr and NATO allies

platforms.name: Bruker CBRNE Detection
platforms.description: Bruker comprehensive CBRNE detection portfolio covering chemical, biological, radiation, nuclear and explosives threats for military and civil defense

platforms.name: DORA (Disease Outbreak Risk Assessment)
platforms.description: Gryphon Scientific/DTRA (now Deloitte) Disease Outbreak Risk Assessment tool for AI-driven country-to-country disease spread prediction and GOF risk analysis

platforms.name: EpiHiper
platforms.description: UVA Biocomplexity Institute / DTRA-funded agent-based pandemic simulation engine for large-scale what-if epidemic scenario modeling

platforms.name: UK Dstl (Porton Down)
platforms.description: UK Defence Science and Technology Laboratory at Porton Down - premier UK biodefense research facility providing CBW defense capabilities to British military

platforms.name: DGA Maîtrise NRBC
platforms.description: French Direction Générale de l'Armement NRBC division providing biological threat detection and CBRN defense capabilities to French armed forces

platforms.name: Bundeswehr CBRN Defence
platforms.description: German Bundeswehr CBRN defence capabilities including Institute of Pharmacology and CBRN reconnaissance for biological threat detection

platforms.name: NATO JCBRN Defence COE
platforms.description: Joint CBRN Defence Centre of Excellence in Brno, Czech Republic - NATO accredited hub for CBRN defence doctrine, training, and capability development

platforms.name: EU HERA
platforms.description: European Commission Health Emergency Preparedness and Response Authority - EU crisis response for cross-border biological threats and MCM procurement

platforms.name: IIBR (Israel Institute for Biological Research)
platforms.description: Israel's premier classified biological research institute at Ness Ziona - national biodefense against intentional attacks and emerging diseases

platforms.name: VECTOR (State Research Center of Virology)
platforms.description: State Research Center of Virology and Biotechnology VECTOR in Novosibirsk - one of two WHO-authorized smallpox repositories and Russian biodefense backbone

platforms.name: Russia 48th CBRN Institute (Sergiev Posad-6)
platforms.description: Russian MoD 48th Central Scientific Research Institute - military biological defense facility with major expansion detected by satellite imagery (2024)

platforms.name: China AMMS (Academy of Military Medical Sciences)
platforms.description: PLA Academy of Military Medical Sciences - China's premier military biodefense institution under Central Military Commission with dual-use capabilities

platforms.name: China CISDCP
platforms.description: China Integrated Surveillance of Disease Control and Prevention - nationwide near-real-time infectious disease reporting system covering 1.4B population

platforms.name: Korea-US Joint Biosurveillance Portal
platforms.description: ROK-US syndromic surveillance system for joint biosurveillance on Korean peninsula including Able Response exercises and KDCA integration

platforms.name: Japan NIID / JSDF CBRN
platforms.description: Japan National Institute of Infectious Diseases and JSDF CBRN capabilities including US-Japan biosurveillance innovation challenge

platforms.name: Australia DSTG CBRN
platforms.description: Defence Science and Technology Group CBRN research program - Australia's military CBRN defense research including biological detection and decontamination

platforms.name: DRDC Suffield (Canada)
platforms.description: Defence Research and Development Canada Suffield Research Centre - Canadian biological threat defense including live-agent training and NATO ally support

platforms.name: India DRDO Biodefense (DFRL/CFEES)
platforms.description: DRDO Defence Food Research Laboratory and Centre for Fire, Explosive & Environment Safety providing biological agent detection and BSL-4 capabilities

platforms.name: Africa CDC AGARI
platforms.description: Africa Genome Archiving for Response and Insight - continent-wide pathogen genomic data sharing platform launched Nov 2025 with biosecurity integration

platforms.name: JEE (WHO Joint External Evaluation)
platforms.description: WHO standardized Joint External Evaluation tool measuring 19 technical areas of IHR compliance including biosafety, biosecurity, and surveillance for 110+ countries

platforms.name: Federal Select Agent Program
platforms.description: CDC/APHIS regulatory program overseeing possession, use and transfer of 67 select biological agents and toxins with security risk potential

platforms.name: JPEO-CBRND Capabilities
platforms.category: US DoD CBRN Defense Acquisition Executive
platforms.description: Joint Program Executive Office for CBRN Defense - manages $1.6B+ Chemical and Biological Defense Program delivering CBRN defense capabilities to all US services

platforms.name: USAID PREDICT
platforms.description: USAID-funded program that discovered 1,200+ novel viruses across 35 countries by sampling 160,000+ animals - foundational to pandemic preparedness

platforms.name: North Korea Bio Program (Assessed)
platforms.description: Assessed DPRK biological weapons program - estimated 13 pathogen agents including anthrax, smallpox, plague; no verified surveillance platform

platforms.name: BEACON (BU/HealthMap)
platforms.description: Biothreats Emergence Analysis and Communications Network - open-source AI+human expert global disease surveillance platform launched April 2025 by Boston University CEID

platforms.name: GPAP (Global Pathogen Analysis Platform)
platforms.description: DTU/Novo Nordisk Foundation funded Global Pathogen Analysis Platform providing free AI-based genomic analytical toolkit for scientists and public health authorities worldwide

platforms.name: PPX (CEPI Pandemic Preparedness Engine)
platforms.description: CEPI-led Pandemic Preparedness Exchange - world's first global AI-factory platform for rapid infectious disease risk identification supporting G7 100 Days Mission

platforms.name: Santé
platforms.category: AI Epidemic Intelligence as a Service
platforms.description: Columbia University health tech startup delivering epidemic intelligence using ML, AI, and geospatial analytics for early detection and prediction

platforms.name: Pandemic PACT Tracker
platforms.description: WHO/Wellcome Trust Pandemic Preparedness Analytical Capacity and Funding Tracker - monitors 20,000+ research grants and global pandemic preparedness investments

platforms.name: DARPA PANACEA
platforms.description: DARPA program developing integrated platform for rapid prediction, synthesis and testing of novel multi-target therapeutics for warfighter physiological needs

platforms.name: DARPA SIGMA+
platforms.description: DARPA SIGMA+ expanding advanced sensor networks for real-time detection of chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear threats in urban environments

platforms.name: JUPITR
platforms.description: Joint US Forces Korea Portal and Integrated Threat Recognition - portable biological detection and biosurveillance network at Korean military installations

platforms.name: EMBD (Enhanced Maritime Biological Detection)
platforms.description: JPEO-CBRND Enhanced Maritime Biological Detection system - upgrade to JBPDS providing automated airborne bio-agent detection and identification for US Navy ships
strengths: Near real-time bio detection at sea

platforms.name: AVCAD (Aerosol Vapor Chemical Agent Detector)
platforms.description: JPEO-CBRND next-generation man-portable chemical/aerosol agent detector by Smiths Detection for joint force CBRN defense with biological detection extension

platforms.name: Chemring TACBIO / JBTDS
platforms.description: Chemring Sensors tactical biological detector and Joint Biological Tactical Detection System for advanced point bio-agent detection with reduced false alarms
strengths: TACBIO 2 patent agreement with DEVCOM CBC
Lowest false alarm rate in class

platforms.name: EpiNow2
platforms.description: Open-source R package for estimating time-varying reproduction number, growth rate and doubling time of infectious disease outbreaks in real-time

platforms.name: Google Earth Engine / Health Map AI
platforms.description: Google Earth Engine combined with AI models for environmental and epidemiological surveillance - used by WHO/FAO for vector-borne disease risk mapping and climate-health forecasting

platforms.name: KIDA Biosecurity Analysis (Korea)
platforms.description: Korea Institute for Defense Analyses biosecurity and CBRN threat assessment division providing policy analysis and defense capability evaluation for ROK military biodefense

platforms.name: EIOS 2.0 (WHO Enhanced)
platforms.category: Next-Gen Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources
platforms.description: WHO upgraded Epidemic Intelligence from Open Sources platform with enhanced AI/ML capabilities for automated event detection across news, social media and official reports in 60+ languages

scope_option_a.name: Global Biosurveillance & Biodefense
scope_option_a.description: Operational detection/response systems. Excludes R packages, pure research programs, hardware-only sensors, policy frameworks.

scope_option_c.name: Pathogen Surveillance Software Benchmark
scope_option_c.description: Usable software platforms only. Excludes military programs, hardware, classified institutes, policy frameworks, R packages.
